IN-CONFIDENCE

SCHEDULE 7  
PRIVACY  

1. DEFINITIONS  

1.1 Definitions: In this Schedule, unless the context otherwise requires, the following definition applies:  

Privacy Breach has the meaning given to that term in the Privacy Act.  

2. PRIVACY  

2.1 Privacy: The Supplier shall comply with the Privacy Laws, and not do anything that would cause [redacted] to breach any Privacy Laws, including:  

(a) ensuring that the Goods and Services provided by the Supplier can be used by [redacted] in compliance with the Privacy Laws;  

(b) having a privacy policy in place that:  

   (i) includes appropriate measures in accordance with good industry practice to avoid the occurrence of, and minimise or mitigate, any Privacy Breach;  

   (ii) includes what to do if there is any Privacy Breach (including in the case of the Supplier, procedures to escalate to [redacted]); and  

   (iii) in the case of the Supplier, complies with all reasonable [redacted] policies and directions notified by [redacted] to the Supplier in relation to privacy; and  

(c) providing all information and assistance reasonably requested by [redacted] in order to facilitate compliance by [redacted] with its obligations under the Privacy Laws, including co-operating with any privacy assessment and completing privacy self-assessments at [redacted] request.  

2.2 Privacy Breach protocol: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier will comply with the following privacy protocol:  

(a) The Supplier shall immediately:  

   (i) notify and escalate any Privacy Breach and/or near miss by the Supplier (or any of its Personnel or Subcontractors) relating to this Contract to [redacted] as soon as it becomes aware of such Privacy Breach or near miss; and  

   (ii) provide all information that [redacted] requests about any Privacy Breach and near miss(es) that could have led to a Privacy Breach that relate to this Contract.  

(b) The Supplier will work together with [redacted] to manage the consequences and implications of the Privacy Breach or near miss, including by:  
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(i) making all such necessary Supplier Personnel available to [redacted] to assist [redacted] to manage the consequences and implications arising from the Privacy Breach or near miss;

(ii) complying with [redacted] issue management procedures;

(iii) following the process in section 113 of the Privacy Act to determine whether a Privacy Breach is a "notifiable privacy breach" (as defined in that Act) and, unless [redacted] notifies the Supplier that [redacted] will make the required notifications in respect of that Privacy Breach, make the required notifications as soon as reasonably practicable; and

(iv) implementing reasonable procedures to prevent such breaches or near misses in the future.

(c) The Supplier will not comment publicly (including to the media) about the Privacy Breach or near miss, without the prior written consent of [redacted] (at its sole discretion).

2.3 Declaration: The Supplier shall, at no less than 6 monthly intervals, provide [redacted] with a signed declaration confirming that the Supplier has complied with this clause 2 and has not exposed [redacted] to any undue risk around privacy requirements in the preceding 6 months.
```

2.2 Renewal: [REDACTED] may extend the term of this Contract the number of times, and for the additional period, as stated in the Contract Details section by giving the Supplier Notice at least 20 Business Days before the then current End Date.

3. VARIATION REQUESTS

clauses.title: 3.1 Variation Request: At any time during the term of this Contract, either
clauses.text: 3.1 Variation Request: At any time during the term of this Contract, either party may request changes or variations to the scope or nature of, or the method of providing:

clauses.title: (a) any Good; and/or
clauses.text: (a) any Good; and/or

clauses.title: (b) any Service,
clauses.text:
(b) any Service,

together with any consequential changes or variations to this Contract, by submitting a Variation Request to the other party in accordance with this clause 3.

clauses.title: 3.2 Contents of Variation Request: Where:
clauses.text: 3.2 Contents of Variation Request: Where:

clauses.title: (a) the party making the Variation Request is [REDACTED] that Variation
clauses.text: (a) the party making the Variation Request is [REDACTED] that Variation Request must outline the proposed change and contain such information as is reasonably necessary to enable the Supplier to respond promptly to [REDACTED] in writing, in the manner contemplated in clause 3.3; or

clauses.title: (b) the party making the Variation Request is the Supplier, the Variation
clauses.text: (b) the party making the Variation Request is the Supplier, the Variation Request will contain a full description of the changes proposed, together with additional information of the type contemplated by clause 3.4.

clauses.title: 3.3 Supplier’s response: The Supplier must, at its own cost, review a
clauses.text: 3.3 Supplier’s response: The Supplier must, at its own cost, review a Variation Request received from [REDACTED] and respond to [REDACTED] by providing [REDACTED] with a submission which contains the information described in clause 3.4 (Submission) within 10 Business Days (or such other period as agreed by the parties in writing) of receipt of such Variation Request.

clauses.title: 3.4 Further information: Each Variation Request from the Supplier, and
clauses.text: 3.4 Further information: Each Variation Request from the Supplier, and each Submission, must include at a minimum:

clauses.title: (a) details as to the feasibility and resource implications (including
clauses.text: (a) details as to the feasibility and resource implications (including [REDACTED] resource implications) of the Variation Request;

clauses.title: (b) details as to any effect which the Variation Request if implemented,
clauses.text: (b) details as to any effect which the Variation Request if implemented, may have on the existing Goods and/or Services including their functionality and performance, existing Service Levels (if applicable), applicable warranties, Charges payable, and any modifications required;

clauses.title: (c) a timeline for implementation and commencement of the Variation
clauses.text: (c) a timeline for implementation and commencement of the Variation Request including any significant interim milestones (as applicable);

clauses.title: (d) any amendments or variations to this Contract required to effect the
clauses.text:
(d) any amendments or variations to this Contract required to effect the Variation Request and, if requested by [REDACTED] a draft variation; and
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

clauses.title: (e) all other relevant information, including information concerning any
clauses.text: (e) all other relevant information, including information concerning any assumptions made by the Supplier.

clauses.title: 3.5 Notification: [REDACTED] will consider the Variation Request or
clauses.text: 3.5 Notification: [REDACTED] will consider the Variation Request or Submission (as the case may be) and notify the Supplier that it:

clauses.title: (a) accepts the Variation Request or Submission, in which case clause
clauses.text: (a) accepts the Variation Request or Submission, in which case clause 3.6 will apply;

clauses.title: (b) wishes to negotiate the Variation Request or Submission, in which
clauses.text: (b) wishes to negotiate the Variation Request or Submission, in which case the parties will then promptly negotiate, and once agreement is reached then clause 3.6 will apply;

clauses.title: (c) withdraws the Variation Request originally made by [REDACTED] to
clauses.text: (c) withdraws the Variation Request originally made by [REDACTED] to which the Submission relates; or

clauses.title: (d) rejects the Variation Request received from the Supplier.
clauses.text:
(d) rejects the Variation Request received from the Supplier.

[REDACTED] may at any time and at the relevant time exercise any of its options under subclauses (a) to (d) above.

clauses.title: 3.6 Amendment: Once agreement has been reached on a Variation Request
clauses.text: 3.6 Amendment: Once agreement has been reached on a Variation Request from the Supplier or a Submission, the parties will negotiate the amendments that need to be made to this Contract based on the terms of the Variation Request or Submission as so agreed. The Supplier will:

clauses.title: (a) ensure that the amendments are, as far as is reasonably appropriate
clauses.text:
(a) ensure that the amendments are, as far as is reasonably appropriate in the circumstances, consistent with the terms of this Contract;

(b) ensure that any new Charges, or amendments to existing Charges, are reasonable, competitive, and directly related to the agreed Variation Request or Submission;

(c) use its best endeavours to mitigate the Charges for any Goods and/or Services that are no longer required as a result of the agreed Variation Request or Submission; and

(d) not impose any unreasonable impacts, consequences, conditions or requirements in relation to the agreed Variation Request or Submission.

To avoid doubt, until such amendment is agreed and signed by the parties, the terms of this Contract shall continue under its then existing terms notwithstanding the agreed Variation Request or Submission.

4. GUARANTEE

4.1 Guarantee: If required in the Contract Details section, the Supplier will provide the following documents on or before the date this Contract is last signed by the parties:

(a) a deed of guarantee in a form acceptable to [REDACTED] duly executed by the Guarantor; and

(b) a legal opinion by a major law firm acceptable to [REDACTED] (acting reasonably) based in the country of incorporation of the Guarantor confirming (subject to standard qualifications) that the deed of guarantee has been properly executed by the
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

Guarantor and is enforceable on its terms against the Guarantor in such country of incorporation of the Guarantor.

5. SUPPLY OF GOODS AND SERVICES

5.1 [REDACTED]’s obligations: [REDACTED] must:

(a) pay the Supplier the Charges for the Goods and Services in accordance with this Contract;

(b) subject to clause 16, allow the Supplier and its Personnel access to [REDACTED] premises and/or [REDACTED] Personnel as reasonably required for the purpose of delivering the Goods and/or Services;

(c) provide the Supplier with any information it has reasonably requested to enable the delivery of the Goods and/or Services; and

(d) within reasonable timeframes, make decisions and give approvals reasonably required by the Supplier to enable delivery of the Goods and/or Services.

5.2 Supplier’s obligations: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier will:

(a) supply to [REDACTED] the Goods and/or Services, and perform its other obligations:

   (i) in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Contract, time being of the essence;

   (ii) with due care, skill and diligence, and to the appropriate professional standard or in accordance with good industry practice as would be expected from a leading supplier in the relevant industry; and

   (iii) in a manner that is culturally appropriate for Māori, Pacific and other ethnic or indigenous groups and respects the personal privacy and dignity of all individuals;

(b) meet [REDACTED] requirements and the required Service Levels, performance standards and quality as described in Schedule 1, and as otherwise reasonably notified by [REDACTED] to the Supplier in writing from time to time;

(c) take full responsibility for the performance of all tasks and activities necessary to provide the Goods and/or Services in accordance with this Contract, other than tasks or activities specifically identified as being [REDACTED] responsibility;

(d) ensure that its Personnel and any Subcontractors have the necessary skills, experience, training and resources to deliver the Goods and/or Services;

(e) provide all Supplier Personnel, documentation, processes, equipment, tools, materials and other resources necessary to perform its obligations under this Contract, including to provide any Goods and/or Services;

(f) without limiting [REDACTED] other rights and remedies, keep [REDACTED] fully advised of the progress of the provision of the Goods and/or Services and changes or possible changes to the scope or timing;
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(g) respond immediately, accurately and adequately to all requests for information made by [REDACTED] in relation to this Contract, including for the purpose of enabling [REDACTED] to comply with its statutory, parliamentary or other reporting obligations;

(h) comply with, and ensure its Personnel and each of its Subcontractors comply with:

    (i) all applicable laws, regulations, rules, codes and standards, and professional codes of conduct or practice, including holding and maintaining all licences, approvals, consents and permits required in order to provide the Goods and/or Services and otherwise to perform its other obligations under this Contract, and must not cause [REDACTED] to be in breach of the same;

    (ii) all of [REDACTED] policies, procedures and access requirements, standards and reasonable notices, directions and instructions relevant to the performance of its obligations under this Contract; and

    (iii) the Supplier Code of Conduct issued by the Procurement System Leader [REDACTED] and any other relevant codes of conduct listed in Schedule 1 or as otherwise notified by [REDACTED] to the Supplier in writing from time to time;

(i) not damage, interfere with or adversely affect the property, operations, reputation or goodwill of [REDACTED] and

(j) immediately notify [REDACTED] in writing of:

    (i) any actual or anticipated issues that could:

        (aa) significantly impact on the Goods, Services, or the Charges; or

        (bb) receive media attention;

    (ii) any change to the criminal record of the Supplier, its Personnel or any Subcontractor involved in the supply of the Goods and/or Services during the term of this Contract; and

    (iii) any breach or likely breach of the Supplier’s obligations under this Contract, including if the Supplier becomes aware of any actual or possible delay in delivery of the Goods and/or Services.

5.3 Employment standards: The Supplier must:

(a) comply with, and ensure that its Personnel and Subcontractors comply with, their obligations under the Employment Relations Act 2000, the Minimum Wage Act 1983, the Wages Protection Act 1983, the Holidays Act 2003 and the Parental Leave and Employment Protection Act 1987; and

(b) report any instances where the Supplier, its Personnel or any Subcontractor is being investigated by the Labour Inspectorate, or where the Supplier, its Personnel or any Subcontractor has:

    (i) identified that it has; and/or

    (ii) been found by the Labour Inspectorate, Employment Relations Authority, or the Employment Court to have,

[REDACTED]
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

breached any of the legislation referenced in subclause (a) above.

5.4 Continuous improvement:

(a) The Supplier will, at its own cost:

    (i) plan for and ensure the continuous improvement and evolution of the Goods and/or Services, including by identifying opportunities to enhance functionality, performance, security, usability and value for money;

    (ii) seek to improve its performance under this Contract on an ongoing basis, including from an efficiency and service delivery perspective; and

    (iii) identify and promptly notify [REDACTED] of any actual or potential improvements, innovations, technologies, or process changes that could:

        (aa) enhance the quality, efficiency or effectiveness of the Goods and/or Services;

        (bb) reduce costs for [REDACTED] or

        (cc) otherwise improve outcomes for [REDACTED] in relation to the Contract,

    provided that the Supplier must not make any changes to any Goods and/or Services without first obtaining [REDACTED] prior written approval.

(b) If [REDACTED] wishes to adopt any proposed improvement, the parties will work together in good faith to agree any changes required to this Contract.

6. WARRANTIES

6.1 Mutual warranties: Each Party represents and warrants that it is authorised to enter into and perform its obligations under this Contract.

6.2 Supplier warranties: The Supplier warrants that:

(a) it has the expertise, capability, facilities, equipment and resources necessary to provide the Goods and/or Services in accordance with this Contract;

(b) the Goods and/or Services will:

    (i) be complete, accurate, free from defects and/or material faults in workmanship and design;

    (ii) conform to, and perform in accordance with, [REDACTED] requirements and the required Service Levels, performance standards and quality as described in Schedule 1, and as otherwise reasonably notified by [REDACTED] to the Supplier in writing from time to time, and all applicable laws;

    (iii) will be delivered to [REDACTED] by the times set out in Schedule 1, or as otherwise notified in writing by [REDACTED] and

    (iv) be fit for their respective purposes as required by [REDACTED]

(c) the Goods:
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(i) will comply with any description and/or sample of the Goods provided by the Supplier, unless [redacted] has agreed otherwise in writing;

(ii) are new and unused, unless [redacted] has agreed otherwise in writing;

(iii) are manufactured, packaged, labelled, stored, transported and handled safely and in accordance with all applicable standards;

(iv) packaged and stored in a manner and in an environment that:
     (aa) protects and maximises the serviceable life of the Goods; and
     (bb) keeps the Goods clean, dry and free from biosecurity hazards.

(v) transported to the Delivery Address specified in Schedule 1 in compliance with any applicable import regulations;

(vi) will, where applicable, be properly installed and fully integrated into [redacted] Operating Environment and/or other systems and property with which they interact;

(vii) will be fully compatible with, and operate satisfactorily in conjunction with, the other Goods and with [redacted] Operating Environment;

(viii) will not damage or detract from the performance of [redacted] Operating Environment and/or other systems and property with which they interact; and

(ix) will be free of any charge, lien, encumbrance or liability, at the earlier of the time of supply to [redacted] and the date [redacted] pays for such Good;

(d) all information provided, or to be provided, to [redacted] by the Supplier under this Contract, including any information it provides with or as part of the Goods and/or Services, is factually correct, complete, not misleading or deceptive and contains no material omissions; and

(e) all documentation provided, or to be provided, to [redacted] by the Supplier under this Contract, including any documentation it provides with or as part of the Goods and/or Services, is clear, concise, written in plain English, consistent in style and format, and error free.

6.3 Third party warranties: The Supplier will, without limiting any of the other warranties given under this Contract, assign to [redacted] or if it is unable to do so, hold for the sole benefit of [redacted] all warranties and guarantees provided by third parties to the Supplier in respect of the provision of any Goods and/or Services under this Contract. Any warranties provided by those third parties are in addition to, and shall not displace, any warranties provided by the Supplier.

6.4 Replacements: The warranties and the other provisions of this Contract apply equally to any replaced Good and/or re-performed Service.

6.5 Warranties in addition to: The warranties set out in this Contract are in addition to, and do not limit, exclude or affect, any other warranties, guarantees, rights or remedies that are implied or imposed by applicable law.

7. PERFORMANCE ISSUES

7.1 Service Improvement Plan: Without limiting any other rights or remedies of [REDACTED], if at any time [REDACTED] reasonably considers that a Performance Issue has occurred, or is likely to occur, including where the Supplier has given [REDACTED] notice of such Performance Issue, [REDACTED] may, by notice to the Supplier setting out its grounds (Performance Notice), require the Supplier to prepare a plan to remedy the Performance Issue (Service Improvement Plan) in accordance with clause 7.2.

7.2 Preparation of Service Improvement Plan: As soon as possible after receipt of a Performance Notice (and in any event within 3 Business Days), the Supplier will, in consultation with [REDACTED], prepare and provide to [REDACTED] a proposed Service Improvement Plan setting out:

(a) full details of the Performance Issue (including its cause(s), expected duration and impact on its obligations under this Contract);

(b) a plan (including milestones) setting out the steps it will take to eliminate, and mitigate the effect of, the Performance Issue and prevent the Performance Issue from reoccurring, as applicable; and

(c) success criteria to determine whether the Performance Issue has been rectified.

7.3 Amendments: The Supplier will promptly (and in any event within 1 Business Day of a request by [REDACTED]) make such amendments to the proposed Service Improvement Plan as [REDACTED] may reasonably require and deliver the amended Service Improvement Plan to [REDACTED] for review.

7.4 Review and approval: [REDACTED] will review the amended Service Improvement Plan and will promptly either:

(a) approve the Service Improvement Plan by written notice to the Supplier; or

(b) notify the Supplier of any further required amendments, to be made in accordance with clause 7.3.

7.5 Implementation: Once [REDACTED] has approved the Service Improvement Plan in accordance with clause 7.4(a), the Supplier will implement the Service Improvement Plan in accordance with its terms and this Contract. The approval and subsequent implementation of the Service Improvement Plan is not a variation to this Contract (except to the extent the parties expressly agree a variation in such Service Improvement Plan), and is without prejudice to, and does not limit, any other rights or remedies [REDACTED] may have under this Contract or at law in respect of any breach of this Contract which led to the creation of the Service Improvement Plan.

7.6 Costs: Unless otherwise agreed with [REDACTED], the Supplier will not charge [REDACTED] any amount for remedying any non-performance of this Contract by the Supplier (including for any repeated or remedial work).

8. REMEDIES FOR FAILURE

8.1 Remedies: If any of the Goods and/or Services are not, in [REDACTED] opinion, provided in accordance with this Contract, including in the event the Supplier breaches any warranty set out in clause 6, then the Supplier must promptly remedy the breach at its cost on receipt of
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

Notice in writing from [REDACTED] requiring the breach to be remedied, and [REDACTED] may, without limiting its other remedies:

(a) give the Supplier Notice to promptly remedy the deficiency at the Supplier’s cost;

(b) withhold any payment due to the Supplier until the deficiency is satisfactorily remedied; and/or

(c) if the Supplier does not remedy the deficiency to [REDACTED] reasonable satisfaction within a reasonable period after [REDACTED] giving Notice under subclause (a) above:

   (i) deduct a reasonable amount from any payment due to the Supplier to reflect the impact of the deficiency;

   (ii) then, for the purpose of remedying such default or non-performance and otherwise protecting [REDACTED] rights and interests under this Contract and/or a Contract (as applicable), and at the cost of the Supplier:

      (aa) appoint any person to assist the Supplier to discharge its obligations under this Contract or (as applicable) a Contract; and/or

      (bb) itself taking control of any Services related to deficiency,

   (iii) and the Supplier will provide all reasonable assistance to that person or [REDACTED] (as applicable); or

   (iv) terminate this Contract under clause 23.4.

8.2 Continuation: To avoid doubt, the Supplier shall continue to provide the Goods and/or Services under this Contract notwithstanding [REDACTED] exercising any of its remedies under this Contract, including under this clause 7.

9. ACCEPTANCE AND REJECTION OF GOODS AND SERVICES

9.1 Acceptance Process: If Schedule 1 specifies that an Acceptance Process for any Good and/or Service is to be carried out, then:

(a) the Supplier must notify [REDACTED] as soon as that Good or Service is ready for the Acceptance Process to be conducted;

(b) the Acceptance Process will be conducted in relation to that Good or Service by the Supplier or [REDACTED] as specified in Schedule 1 to ensure that Good or Service meets the relevant Acceptance Criteria; and

(c) if [REDACTED] is undertaking the Acceptance Process, the Supplier will give [REDACTED] such information and assistance as [REDACTED] reasonably requires in relation to undertaking the Acceptance Process.

9.2 Failure of Acceptance Process: Without limiting [REDACTED] other rights and remedies, if any Good or Service fails any Acceptance Process:

(a) the party undertaking the relevant Acceptance Process will promptly notify the other party of the failure within 3 Business Days of completion of the relevant
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

Acceptance Process, specifying the particular respects in which the Good or Service failed to satisfy the Acceptance Process;

(b) the failures will be promptly rectified by the Supplier at its own cost within 10 Business Days of notification of the failure (unless otherwise agreed by the parties in writing), including by:

   (i) repairing or replacing the Goods; and/or

   (ii) reperforming the Services;

(c) the Supplier must notify ███ as soon as the relevant Good or Service is ready to be retested;

(d) subject to clause 9.3, the Supplier must continue to rectify any failures it discovers or are notified to it and the Acceptance Process must be repeated until all Acceptance Criteria are met; and

(e) the Supplier will not charge ███ for any services required to rectify or remedy any failures and re-conduct any Acceptance Process.

9.3 Consecutive failures: If:

(a) the Acceptance Criteria are not met after the second time any Acceptance Process has been carried out for the same failure; or

(b) ███ is not satisfied with the Supplier’s progress in rectifying the failures, including by repairing or replacing the Goods and/or reperforming the Services,

███ may, without prejudice to any of its other rights and remedies:

(c) Accept the relevant Good or Service subject to:

   (i) a reduction in the relevant Charges; and/or

   (ii) such conditions as ███ may specify by notice in writing to the Supplier;

(d) reject the relevant Good or Service, in which case clause 9.4 will apply; or

(e) arrange for the Good or Service to be repaired, replaced or reperformed (as applicable) by a third party, in which case the Supplier will reimburse all costs and expenses incurred by ███ in doing so.

9.4 Rejection: If the Supplier receives a Notice from ███ rejecting the Good or Service, it must, at the Supplier’s cost:

(a) remove any rejected Good or Service from ███ premises at its own risk within 15 Business Days of Notice of rejection, and if the Supplier fails to do so, ███ may return the Good and/or Service and recover from the Supplier any cost and expense incurred; and

(b) do either of the following options that ███ elects in its Notice of rejection:

   (i) provide a full refund of the Charges paid for the rejected Good and/or Service, within 10 Business Days of ███ electing to receive a refund; or
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(ii) provide a credit for the Charges paid for the rejected Good and/or Services to be applied against any fees payable to the Supplier (including under this Contract).

9.5 Acceptance Certificates:

(a) If a Good or Service passes the Acceptance Process, [REDACTED] must promptly issue an Acceptance Certificate in relation to that Good or Service.

(b) For the avoidance of doubt, the issue of an Acceptance Certificate under the provisions of this clause 9 will not relieve the Supplier of either the performance of its obligations or from any breach of its obligations, or of any warranty, under this Contract.

9.6 Other acceptance: Any Good or Service that is not subject to an Acceptance Process is subject to [REDACTED] approval. If [REDACTED] does not approve the Good or Service, the Supplier must make any change that is reasonably required by [REDACTED] and re-provide the Good or Service to [REDACTED] for approval.

9.7 No deemed acceptance: For the avoidance of doubt:

(a) no act or omission on the part of [REDACTED] in connection with this clause 9 will be considered to be deemed Acceptance of any Good or Service;

(b) unless otherwise agreed by the parties, Acceptance of a Good or Service does not occur until such time as [REDACTED] issues an Acceptance Certificate in respect of that Good or Service; and

(c) [REDACTED] shall not be deemed to have Accepted a Good or Service by reason of use of the Good or receipt of the Service (or part thereof) by [REDACTED].

9.8 No limitation: [REDACTED] rights in this clause 9 are in addition to, and do not limit, any other rights or remedies [REDACTED] may have.

10. TITLE AND RISK

10.1 Title: Title in the Goods passes to [REDACTED] on the earlier of:

(a) the date [REDACTED] has paid the Charges for those Goods; and

(b) the date those Goods have been delivered.

10.2 Risk: Risk in the Goods passes to [REDACTED] on issue of an Acceptance Certificate by [REDACTED] unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties.

10.3 Rejected Goods: Title and risk in any Goods rejected by [REDACTED] under clause 9 will pass back to the Supplier as follows:

(a) if [REDACTED] has paid the Charges for those rejected Goods, once the Supplier has provided a refund or credit in accordance with clause 9.4(b); and

(b) in all other cases, when the Goods are collected from the place to which they were delivered.
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

10.4 Replaced Goods: Ownership of any Goods replaced by the Supplier under clause 9 will pass back to the Supplier once the Supplier or the relevant third party (as applicable) has delivered the replacement Goods.

11. MILESTONES

11.1 Milestones:

(a) The Supplier shall meet each Milestone by the corresponding Milestone Date in accordance with the Acceptance Criteria, as specified in Schedule 1.

(b) Each Milestone is complete when [REDACTED] has advised the Supplier in writing that [REDACTED] is satisfied that the Supplier has successfully completed the Milestone.

(c) [REDACTED] may extend 1 or more Milestone Dates at any time by giving notice in writing to the Supplier.

11.2 Milestone Delay: Without limiting any other rights or remedies of [REDACTED], if the Supplier fails, or is likely to fail, to complete a Milestone by the relevant Milestone Date (a Milestone Delay):

(a) the Supplier shall, immediately:

(i) provide full details of the Milestone Delay to [REDACTED] in writing, including:

(aa) how the Supplier intends to complete the relevant Milestone by the Milestone Date or as soon as possible thereafter;

(bb) the relevant timeframes for such completion; and

(cc) any other details requested by [REDACTED] and [REDACTED]

(ii) take all steps available to it to avoid and minimise the effects of the Milestone Delay, together with any other steps required by [REDACTED] (including applying additional resources and/or Personnel to complete the relevant Milestone by the Milestone Date or as soon as possible thereafter); and

(b) the Parties shall, if requested by [REDACTED], meet to review in good faith the reasons for the Milestone Delay.

11.3 Failure to complete a Milestone:

(a) Subject to subclause (b) below, if the Supplier has failed to complete a Milestone by the relevant Milestone Date, the Supplier will pay [REDACTED] any actual costs incurred by [REDACTED] as a consequence of the Milestone Delay, including reasonable costs arising from any remedial or other steps that [REDACTED] takes to mitigate the impact of such failure.

(b) Any failure or delay by the Supplier to perform its obligations under this Contract will be excused to the extent that the failure was caused by:

(i) any failure by [REDACTED] to perform its obligations under this Contract; or

(ii) an Extraordinary Event, in accordance with (and without limiting) clause 31.

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

12. CHARGES AND PAYMENT

12.1 Maximum amount: The Charges set out in Schedule 1 are the total maximum amount payable by [REDACTED] to the Supplier for delivery of the Goods and Services. Where expressly agreed and incurred in accordance with Schedule 1, Charges include Expenses. All other costs, disbursements and other expenses incurred by the Supplier in relation to this Contract are included in the Charges.

12.2 Milestones: If the Charges are expressed to be payable upon the achievement of 1 or more Milestone(s), then the Supplier shall not invoice [REDACTED] until after the achievement of those Milestone(s).

12.3 Rate Card: Where a Rate Card has been agreed, the Supplier agrees that the labour rate used to calculate the Charges shall not exceed the amounts set out in the Rate Card, for the corresponding Supplier Personnel role or level of seniority.

12.4 No other amounts payable: The Charges are the only amounts payable by [REDACTED] for the Goods and Services and all other related matters.

12.5 No payment for remedial work: The Supplier will not charge [REDACTED] any amount for remedying any non-performance of this Contract by the Supplier, including for any repeated or remedial work.

12.6 Payment is not acceptance: Payment by [REDACTED] in respect of any invoice will not be considered to be acceptance of any Goods and Services provided prior to the date of the invoice or the date of payment, unless specified otherwise.

12.7 Invoice: Unless otherwise agreed in Schedule 1, the Supplier will invoice [REDACTED] for the Charges in respect of the Goods and Services on or by the 5th Business Day following the month in which Goods were delivered and/or Services were provided (as applicable). If the Supplier fails to provide [REDACTED] with an invoice within 6 months of the agreed date for invoicing, the Supplier is deemed to have waived any right to that payment from [REDACTED]. Each invoice must:

(a) be a Tax Invoice;

(b) be sent directly to [REDACTED] via email to [REDACTED] or as otherwise directed by [REDACTED];

(c) include all information reasonably required to enable [REDACTED] to validate the claim for payment including:

   (i) any purchase order number and/or contract number applicable to the invoice;

   (ii) reference to this Contract (under which the invoice is issued);

   (iii) the Charges for and description of each Good and Service to which the invoice relates;

   (iv) in respect of any Charges on a time and rate basis, the relevant times and rates on which the Charge is based, including a breakdown of time spent by each resource and the associated rate and total Charge for each resource; and
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(v) full details of all Expenses and/or disbursements incurred that are included in the invoice; and

(d) be marked for the attention of [REDACTED] Accounts Payable and [REDACTED] Contract Manager.

12.8 Payment: Subject to clause 8.1, 12.9, 12.10, 12.11 and 12.13, [REDACTED] will use its best endeavours to pay an invoice that complies with clause 12.7 within 10 Business Days of receiving the invoice. If [REDACTED] cannot meet this timeframe, [REDACTED] will pay that invoice by:

(a) the 20th calendar day of the month, if the invoice is received on or before the 5th Business Day of the month; or

(b) the 20th calendar day of the following month, if the invoice is received after the 5th Business Day of the month.

12.9 Set off: [REDACTED] may deduct from or set-off any amount the Supplier owes to it, howsoever arising under this Contract, against any amount or other payment that is or may become owing by [REDACTED] to the Supplier.

12.10 Dispute:

21. RESOLVING DISPUTES

21.1 Resolution of Disputes: Each Party shall, in good faith, use all commercial endeavours to:

(a) resolve any dispute, disagreement, question or difference (Dispute) that arises between them in relation to this Contract;

(b) minimise the impact of any Dispute; and
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(c) ensure that where a Dispute is reasonably foreseeable, it is dealt with at a sufficiently early stage so as to minimise its effect on the Parties.

21.2 Dispute: If either Party has a Dispute concerning any matter relating to this Contract, then that Party must give written notice of the Dispute to the other Party setting out the details of the Dispute, naming its representative for negotiations, and requiring that the Dispute be determined in accordance with this clause 21 (Dispute Notice).

21.3 Negotiation: Upon receipt of a Dispute Notice:

(a) each Party will direct its named representative(s) to meet promptly and as many times as necessary to negotiate to resolve the Dispute; and

(b) if the Dispute is not resolved under subclause (a) above, the Dispute will be referred immediately to the chief executives (or equivalent) of each Party for the purpose of resolving the Dispute on mutually acceptable terms.

21.4 Mediation: If the Dispute is not resolved through negotiation after 5 Business Days of referral to the chief executives in accordance with clause 21.3(b), then, unless both Parties agree otherwise, either Party may refer (by giving written notice to the other Party) the Dispute to mediation to be held in Wellington as soon as possible (Mediation Notice). The mediation shall be conducted by a single mediator (being a New Zealand resident) who, unless otherwise agreed between the Parties within 5 Business Days of the giving of the Mediation Notice, shall be appointed by the chair of Resolution Institute. In the event of any such submission to mediation:

(a) the mediator will not be deemed to be acting as an expert or arbitrator;

(b) the mediation will be conducted on the terms of the Resolution Institute Mediation Rules and the mediator will determine the procedure and timetable for the mediation. Both Parties agree to adhere to the mediator’s decisions on the operation of the mediation process;

(c) the cost of the mediation will be shared equally between the Parties, or if they do not agree to sharing such cost equally, at such cost determined by the Resolution Institute; and

(d) the mediation will terminate 1 month after the appointment of the mediator, unless both Parties agree otherwise.

21.5 Arbitration: If the Dispute is not resolved through mediation within 15 Business Days, or such later time as the Parties may agree, from the date of the Mediation Notice, then either Party may refer the Dispute to arbitration, by notice in writing to the other Party (Arbitration Notice). The arbitration will be determined by a sole arbitrator (being a New Zealand resident) as soon as possible in Wellington, New Zealand. If the Parties cannot agree on an arbitrator within 5 Business Days of the giving of the Arbitration Notice, the arbitrator will be appointed by the President of the Arbitrators and Mediators’ Institute of New Zealand or their nominee. The arbitration will be conducted in accordance with the Arbitration Act 1996. The costs of the arbitrator shall (subject to any award by the arbitrator) be borne equally by the Parties to the Dispute.

21.6 Condition precedent: Subject to clause 21.9 and any right any Party may have to apply to a court for any interim or preliminary relief in respect of the Dispute, completion or termination of the negotiation as set out in clause 21.3 and completion or termination of the mediation as
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

set out in clause 21.4 shall be a condition precedent to the commencement of arbitration under clause 21.5.

21.7 Restrictions on use of information: The Parties acknowledge that the purpose of any exchange of information or documents or the making of any offer of settlement pursuant to clause 21.3 is to attempt to settle the Dispute between the Parties. No Party may use any information or documents obtained solely by reason of clause 21.3 for any purpose other than in an attempt to settle the Dispute.

21.8 Continuation of performance: Notwithstanding the Dispute, the Parties will continue performing their respective obligations under this Contract to the maximum extent possible while the Dispute is being resolved (having regard to the nature of the Dispute, with the exception that payments may be withheld to the extent that they are disputed).

21.9 Urgent relief: Nothing in this clause 21 will preclude either Party from taking immediate steps to seek urgent equitable relief before a New Zealand court.

(b) commits a number of breaches that collectively constitute a material breach or have a material effect on the performance of this Contract by the breaching Party and the breaching Party has failed to remedy the breaches to the non-breaching Party’s satisfaction within 15 Business Days after receiving notice from the non-breaching Party specifying the breaches or failures and requiring remedy; or

(c) has acted fraudulently.

(i) for the disclosure of any [REDACTED] Data claimed by the requestor to be held, stored or processed by, or under the control of, the Supplier under this Contract;

Accordingly, in addition to any claim for damages and any other remedies available at law or equity, the non-breaching Party may seek specific performance or injunctive relief against any breach or threatened breach by the other Party, its Personnel, agents or Subcontractors of this clause 26. Each Party undertakes to provide the other Party with any assistance possible in any such action against any of that first Party’s Personnel, agents or Subcontractors.

27. CHILDREN’S ACT 2014

27.1 Supplier obligations: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, where the Supplier provides services to or in connection with any children for the purposes of the Children’s Act 2014, the Supplier will:
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(a) have, maintain and give effect to a child protection policy which accords with section 19 of the Children’s Act 2014;

(b) review and update its child protection policy at least every three years to ensure it remains current and appropriate;

(c) carry out, and respond appropriately to the results of, all vetting and safety checks required under Part 3 of the Children’s Act 2014 and the Children’s (Requirements for Safety Checks of Children’s Workers) Regulations 2015;

(d) repeat the vetting and safety checks described at subclause (c) above at least every three years to ensure its Personnel remain eligible for roles involving the care and supervision of young people or young adults; and

(e) upon request, provide [REDACTED]

   (i) a copy of its initial and any updated child protection policy;

   (ii) confirmation that it has carried out and responded appropriately to all vetting and safety checks in accordance with subclauses (c) and (d) above; and

30. BUSINESS CONTINUITY

30.1 Business continuity: The Supplier shall take reasonable precautions to prevent and mitigate any disaster or crisis (including any financial crisis or stress) or any other event or circumstance that may interfere with the availability or operation of the Goods or Services (each a Trigger Event). Without limiting the foregoing, the Supplier shall maintain at all times, test and, where required, implement, an appropriate business continuity and disaster recovery plan or plans (the Business Continuity Plan) to address a Trigger Event. The Business Continuity Plan will address, at a minimum:

(a) continued ability to access and retrieve ███ Data and any backups of that ███ Data;

(b) continued ability to access up to date and accurate Documentation; and

(c) retention of key resources required to ensure the provision of all Goods and Services.

30.2 Access to Business Continuity Plan: The Supplier will provide ███ with a copy of the Business Continuity Plan if requested by ███.

30.3 Plan implementation: Upon the occurrence of a Trigger Event, the Supplier shall promptly implement the relevant portion or portions of the Business Continuity Plan and shall notify ███ of such implementation.

30.4 Plan development: The Supplier will release such additional information as may be reasonably necessary to allow ███ to develop and maintain its own business continuity plan to work in concert with the Supplier’s Business Continuity Plan and shall provide reasonable assistance to ███ to develop and maintain such plan or plans.

30.5 Business Continuity Plan review:

(a) The Supplier must review, test and, if necessary to comply with clause 30.1, update the Business Continuity Plan on an annual basis, and share with ███ the results from such review and testing.

(b) ███ may (but shall not be required to), on an annual basis, review and test the Business Continuity Plan for compliance with clause 30.1, in which case the Supplier shall provide all reasonable assistance to ███ to do so.

(c) ███ may, from time to time, require the Supplier to update the Business Continuity Plan to ensure compliance with clause 30.1.

30.6 No limitation: Nothing in this clause 30, including providing the Business Continuity Plan to ███ or including any changes ███ requests, will relieve the Supplier of liability for any breach of its obligations, or of any warranty, under this Contract.

Variation Request means a request by either party to change or vary the scope or nature of the Goods and/or Services, in accordance with the process and requirements set out in clause 3.

Variation Request Process means the process set out in clause 3.

SCHEDULE 4

PART A – HEALTH AND SAFETY (LOW RISK)

clauses.title: 1. DEFINITIONS
clauses.text:
1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 In this Schedule, unless the context otherwise requires, the following definitions apply:

Control Measures has the meaning given to that term in regulation 3 of the HSW (GRWM) Regulations 2016.

HSW Act means the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015.

HSW (GRWM) Regulations 2016 means the Health and Safety at Work (General Risk and Workplace Management) Regulations 2016.

HSW Laws means the HSW Act and includes all regulations made under that Act (including but not limited to the HSW (GRWM) Regulations 2016), and any other health and safety-related legislation relevant to the Supplier’s supply of Goods and/or Services to ██████.

Notifiable Event has the meaning given to that term in section 25 of the HSW Act.

PCBU means a person conducting a business or undertaking within the meaning of section 17 of the HSW Act.

Worker has the meaning given to that term in section 19 of the HSW Act.

WorkSafe NZ means WorkSafe New Zealand.

2. COMPLIANCE WITH HEALTH & SAFETY LEGISLATION AND DIRECTIONS

2.1 Health, safety and security: The Supplier must:

(a) consult, cooperate and coordinate with ██████ regarding the Parties’ overlapping obligations under, and what is required from the Supplier to assist ██████ to comply with, the HSW Laws as they relate to, or affect, this Contract;

(b) comply with, and ensure that its Personnel, Workers and Subcontractors comply with, their obligations under the HSW Laws as it relates to, or affects, this Contract, including:

   (i) duties of a PCBU under sections 36 – 43 of the HSW Act;

   (ii) duties relating to the identification of hazards and implementation of Control Measures under the HSW (GRWM) Regulations 2016; and

   (iii) duties relating to the keeping of records under section 57 of the HSW Act; and
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(c) comply with, and ensure its Personnel, Workers and Subcontractors comply with, all reasonable directions of [redacted] relating to health, safety, and security, as notified to the Supplier from time to time.

3. HEALTH AND SAFETY RISK MANAGEMENT

3.1 Health and safety risk management: The Supplier must:

(a) maintain a general health and safety policy and practices that are appropriate to the nature of the Goods and/or Services provided to [redacted];

(b) comply with, and ensure its Personnel, Workers and Subcontractors comply with, the Supplier’s health and safety policy and practices;

(c) identify, control and regularly review all risks to health and safety;

(d) keep and regularly update a register of all health and safety risks it has identified in relation to its provision of Goods and/or Services, clearly identifying all relevant risks and including appropriate Control Measures to ensure all identified risks are mitigated;

(e) upon request, provide [redacted] with a copy of the register of all health and safety risks; and

(f) ensure that any risks to health and safety in subcontracted activities are being appropriately identified, reviewed and mitigated.

clauses.title: 4. NOTIFYING EVENTS TO WORKSAFE NZ OR OTHER RELEVANT REGULATOR
clauses.text:
4. NOTIFYING EVENTS TO WORKSAFE NZ OR OTHER RELEVANT REGULATOR

4.1 Notifiable Events:

(a) The Supplier will ensure that all Notifiable Events occurring during delivery of the Goods and/or Services, or that otherwise relate to or affect this Contract, are duly notified to WorkSafe NZ (or any other relevant regulator) in accordance with the requirements of the HSW Laws.

(b) Where the Supplier or its Personnel, Workers or Subcontractors, notifies WorkSafe NZ (or any other relevant regulator) of a Notifiable Event in accordance with subclause (a) above, the Supplier must:

(i) report the Notifiable Event to [redacted] within one Business Day of becoming aware of the Notifiable Event; and

(ii) provide to [redacted] a written summary of the Notifiable Event and corrective actions identified.

5. SUBCONTRACTORS

5.1 Subcontractor requirements: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier:
```

(b) must ensure that all Subcontractors are managing risks to health and safety at all times.

6. HEALTH AND SAFETY AUDIT AND INSPECTION

6.1 Audit and inspection: Without limiting its other rights and remedies under this Contract, ███ or any third party appointed by ███ may:

(a) request information and/or documentation from the Supplier relating to:

clauses.text: (i) an audit of the Supplier’s health and safety systems as they relate to this Contract; and/or

clauses.text:

6.2 Supplier obligations: The Supplier must promptly provide:

clauses.title: (a) the requested information and/or documentation in the format specified
clauses.text:

(b) ███ with access and assistance in respect of any audit or inspection carried out under subclause 6.1 above.

6.3 Application to Subcontractors: This clause 6 applies to each Subcontractor of the Supplier as if that Subcontractor were the Supplier. The Supplier must ensure that each Subcontractor is bound by enforceable obligations that give effect to this clause 6.

6.4 Remedying non-compliance: Without limiting any of ███ other rights or remedies, if any non-compliance or concern is identified as part of an audit under this clause 6, the Supplier must act in good faith to resolve the issue and take any reasonable remedial steps required by ███.

clauses.title: INFORMATION AND DATA REQUIREMENTS
clauses.text:
INFORMATION AND DATA REQUIREMENTS

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 Definitions: In this Schedule, unless the context otherwise requires, the following definitions apply:

clauses.title: Disabling Code means any program code or programming instructions, or any d
clauses.text: Disabling Code means any program code or programming instructions, or any device, which may damage, interfere with, impair or otherwise adversely affect a party’s operations, prevent or hinder access to any program or [REDACTED] Data, or enable unauthorised access to any program or [REDACTED] Data, impair or disrupt the operation of any program or the reliability of any [REDACTED] Data (whether by rearranging, altering or erasing the program or [REDACTED] Data in whole or part or otherwise), including malicious code, trojan horses, worms, spyware, malware, computer viruses, logic bombs, backdoors and other similar things.

clauses.title: Industry Best Practice means that degree of skill, diligence, prudence, fo
clauses.text: Industry Best Practice means that degree of skill, diligence, prudence, foresight and judgement which would be reasonably and ordinarily expected from a skilled, reasonable and experienced operator in the information technology industry in the same or similar circumstances, applying the standards, practices and policies that any competent supplier of the Goods and/or Services would be expected to comply with, with reference to industry accepted standards and practices.

clauses.title: Māori Data means data, information or knowledge (including mātauranga Māor
clauses.text: Māori Data means data, information or knowledge (including mātauranga Māori) that is about, from or connected to Māori. It also includes data about population, place, culture and environment, and Personal Information about individuals.

clauses.title: Multi-source Environment means the environment in which [REDACTED] procure
clauses.text: Multi-source Environment means the environment in which [REDACTED] procures or obtains its information technology communication goods and services from a range of information technology, telecommunications, network and equipment suppliers. To avoid doubt, [REDACTED] may provide some of these goods and services itself.

clauses.title: Open Source Software means any software which is licensed under any form o
clauses.text: Open Source Software means any software which is licensed under any form of open source licence meeting the Open Source Initiative’s Open Source Definition found at [REDACTED].

clauses.title: Permitted Purposes has the meaning given to that term in clause 3.1(a) of t
clauses.text: Permitted Purposes has the meaning given to that term in clause 3.1(a) of this Schedule.

clauses.title: Security Breach has the meaning given to that term in clause 4.5 of this S
clauses.text: Security Breach has the meaning given to that term in clause 4.5 of this Schedule.

clauses.title: 2. [REDACTED] DATA
clauses.text: 2. [REDACTED] DATA

clauses.title: 2.1 Ownership of [REDACTED] Data: Notwithstanding any other provision in th
clauses.text: 2.1 Ownership of [REDACTED] Data: Notwithstanding any other provision in this Contract, the Supplier acknowledges that it does not own any [REDACTED] Data.

clauses.title: 2.2 Supplier access to [REDACTED] Data: Subject to the Supplier complying w
clauses.text:
2.2 Supplier access to [REDACTED] Data: Subject to the Supplier complying with clause 2.3 and [REDACTED] security and confidentiality requirements, policies and procedures, [REDACTED] will provide the Supplier such access to [REDACTED] Data as is reasonably required for the Supplier to provide the Goods and/or Services in accordance with this Contract.

IN-CONFIDENCE

clauses.title: 2.3 Treatment of [REDACTED] Data: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obl
clauses.text: 2.3 Treatment of [REDACTED] Data: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier must:

clauses.title: (a) not, without the prior written approval of [REDACTED] in each instance,
clauses.text:
(a) not, without the prior written approval of [REDACTED] in each instance, use [REDACTED] Data other than for the purposes of providing the Goods and Services under this Contract;

(b) keep the [REDACTED] Data (including any backup archives of the [REDACTED] Data) in the possession or control of the Supplier secure, managed and protected, and implement and maintain appropriate measures to prevent the loss of, unauthorised access, use, processing, disclosure, accidental loss or destruction of or damage to, and any other misuse relating to the [REDACTED] Data;

(c) ensure that all [REDACTED] Data is stored in a secure manner, using encryption or equivalent security measures appropriate to the sensitivity of the [REDACTED] Data, and will not:
   (i) store, or allow to be stored, the [REDACTED] Data, on servers or any other data storage equipment on which any other person’s data is stored; and
   (ii) combine, or allow to be combined, the [REDACTED] Data with the Supplier’s, or any other person’s, data in any way;

(d) ensure that any transfer or transmission of [REDACTED] Data (whether internally or externally) is carried out using secure methods that protect the integrity and confidentiality of the [REDACTED] Data in transit;

(e) not copy, reproduce, publish, sell, let, modify, extract, or otherwise part with possession of, the whole or any part of the [REDACTED] Data, or relay or disseminate the same to any third party;

3. PERSONAL INFORMATION

3.1 Access to Personal Information: The Supplier will use, access, collect, store, process or transmit Personal Information only:

(b) to the extent that the Personal Information is described in Schedule 1 and is reasonably necessary to achieve the Permitted Purposes; and

(c) in the manner described in Schedule 1.

3.2 Permitted Purposes:

(a) Subject to subclause (b) below, the Supplier must not use the Personal Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purposes.

(b) Any proposed use of Personal Information by the Supplier for a purpose other than a Permitted Purpose must not occur unless:

   (i) [REDACTED] has first been notified in writing;

   (ii) [REDACTED] has provided its prior written agreement to the proposed additional use; and

   (iii) this Contract has been amended to reflect the change.

3.3 Protection of Personal Information: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier must:

(a) ensure that the Personal Information is protected against loss, access, use, modification, or disclosure that is not authorised by [REDACTED];

(b) not use or disclose the Personal Information to any person or organisation for any purpose other than as expressly permitted under Schedule 1; and

(c) treat all Personal Information in accordance with:

   (i) the Privacy Laws;

   (ii) [REDACTED] data handling policies (as notified); and

   (iii) the requirements set out in Schedule 1.

4.3 Cyber security: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, when accessing [REDACTED] Operating Environment, the Supplier must

(a) access only those systems and areas authorised in writing by [REDACTED] and only for the purposes explicitly permitted under this Contract;

(b) not introduce or connect any portable storage devices (including magnetic media, USB drives or external hard drives) into [REDACTED] Operating Environment without [REDACTED] prior written approval;

(c) not use, connect to or permit the use of any externally hosted (cloud-based) file storage, data processing, or data transfer system in connection with [REDACTED] Operating Environment without [REDACTED] prior written approval; and

(d) not engage in any activity that may compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of [REDACTED] Operating Environment or [REDACTED] Data and take all reasonable steps to prevent such threats, consistent with Industry Best Practice.

4.4 Disabling Code:

(a) The Supplier will take all appropriate precautions to prevent the introduction of Disabling Code to [REDACTED] (including [REDACTED] Operating Environment) when providing the Goods and Services, including:

(i) ensuring that no Disabling Code is coded or introduced into [REDACTED] Operating Environment;

(ii) ensuring that all systems, software and technologies used to provide the Goods, Services and to communicate with [REDACTED] are kept secure in accordance with Industry Best Practice and employ best-in-class and up-to-date versions of reputable third-party anti-virus software that is capable of the detection, shielding and inoculation of known Disabling Code;

(iii) checking files for Disabling Code using anti-virus software; and

(iv) taking appropriate action when Disabling Code is discovered, such as quarantining the file.

(b) Without limiting [REDACTED] other rights and remedies under this Contract, if the Supplier becomes aware that any Disabling Code has been introduced into [REDACTED] Operating Environment, the Supplier must:

(i) immediately report that fact to [REDACTED] and provide all information reasonably requested by [REDACTED] in relation to the Disabling Code, its manner of introduction and the effect the Disabling Code has had or is likely to have;

(ii) take all necessary remedial action to eliminate the Disabling Code and prevent reoccurrence (including implementing appropriate processes to prevent further occurrences) and rectify any consequences (to the extent that they are capable of rectification); and

(iii) if the Disabling Code causes a loss of operational efficiency or loss of [REDACTED] Data, provide all necessary assistance to [REDACTED] to mitigate the losses and restore the efficiency and/or data as quickly as practicable.

4.5 Security breaches: If the Supplier becomes aware of a security incident related in any way to this Contract, any actual, threatened or suspected cyberattack or other cyber incident, or any breach in the confidentiality, availability or integrity of [REDACTED] Data or [REDACTED] Operating Environment (in each case, a Security Breach), the Supplier must:

(a) immediately notify [REDACTED] of the Security Breach;

(b) to the extent the Security Breach arises from or in connection with any act or omission of the Supplier, as soon as practicable:

    (i) identify the cause of the Security Breach and do all things within its power to remedy the Security Breach and its consequences; and

    (ii) provide [REDACTED] with a report detailing the cause of, and steps taken to remedy, the Security Breach, and certifying that the Supplier has, and all applicable Subcontractors have, taken all reasonable action to prevent any recurrence of the Security Breach;

(c) in any event, assist and work together with [REDACTED] to investigate, defend against, address, respond to, and mitigate adverse impacts from, such Security Breach;

(d) subject to subclause (e) below and without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, keep confidential the occurrence (or threatened or suspected occurrence) of any Security Breach, to the extent possible under law; and

(e) if [REDACTED] requests, assist [REDACTED] to notify affected individuals where [REDACTED] determines such notification should occur.

Nothing in this clause 4.5 limits or restricts any rights and remedies available to [REDACTED] under this Contract.

5.1 Multi-source Environment: The Supplier acknowledges that it may be providing the Goods and Services in a Multi-source Environment alongside Third Party Providers, and acknowledges and agrees that it must:

(a) act co-operatively and in good faith with [REDACTED] and any Third Party Providers to:

    (i) deliver the Goods and Services, and related products efficiently and as required;

    (ii) give priority to resolving problems or issues affecting the Goods and Services before seeking to allocate or apportion responsibility to a Third Party Provider for such resolution;

    (iii) ensure [REDACTED] rights under this Contract and its agreements with Third Party Providers are not affected; and

    (iv) not interfere with any Third Party Provider’s ability to provide goods and/or services;

(b) prior to the use of third party software or Open Source Software, confirm to [REDACTED] that such third party software or Open Source Software is generally available for licensing and is generally supported in the New Zealand market on reasonably standard industry terms for a reasonable charge; and

SCHEDULE 6  
USE OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE  

1. DEFINITIONS  

1.1 Definitions: In this Schedule, unless the context otherwise requires, the following definition applies:  

AI means artificial intelligence technologies, including machine learning algorithms, automated decision-making systems, natural language processing models, or generative artificial intelligence tools.  

2. USE OF AI  

2.1 AI use and warranty: The Supplier warrants that:  

(a) as at the Start Date, the Goods and Services incorporate only the AI technologies expressly disclosed to [REDACTED] in writing (if applicable), and no other AI technologies are utilised; and  

(b) such use of AI as described in subclause (a) above complies with all obligations set out in this Contract.  

2.2 Future or changed use of AI:  

(a) If the Supplier proposes to:  

(i) incorporate AI into the Goods and/or Services beyond what has been disclosed as at the Start Date; or  

(ii) change the use, function, scope, or underlying technology of any AI currently used in the Goods and/or Services,  

then the Supplier will provide prior written Notice to [REDACTED] detailing:  

(iii) a description of the AI technology to be introduced or changed, including its purpose and function;  

(iv) the impact of any new or changed AI on the Goods and/or Services, including any changes in performance, accuracy, or reliability;  

(v) a detailed explanation of any data processing, storage, or sharing that may occur as a result of the new or changed AI use;  

(vi) all necessary documentation to enable [REDACTED] to conduct its own due diligence, including but not limited to security assessments, compliance reports, ethical impact analysis, and regulatory considerations; and  

(vii) any additional measures proposed by the Supplier to ensure compliance with existing obligations under this Contract, including data protection and confidentiality requirements.  
```

```
IN-CONFIDENCE

(b) [REDACTED] shall have a reasonable period, not less than 30 Business Days, to assess the Supplier’s notification and determine whether to approve or reject the proposed new or changed AI integration into the Goods and/or Services.

2.3 [REDACTED] approval: If [REDACTED] elects to approve the new or changed use of AI proposed under clause 2.2, the following provisions shall apply:

(a) The Supplier must ensure that:

   (i) no [REDACTED] Data is used for AI training, model refinement or for any other purpose beyond the expressly agreed scope of the Goods and Services; and

   (ii) any [REDACTED] data processed in connection with AI is held within secure, access-controlled environments and is not made available to the public or to any third party without [REDACTED] prior written approval (at its sole discretion).

(b) All existing obligations set out in this Contract (including in relation to privacy, confidentiality, and data protection) shall continue to apply without modification.

(c) The Supplier must maintain transparency regarding AI-generated outputs and ensure that all AI-influenced decisions remain subject to human oversight where necessary.

(d) The Supplier must provide ongoing reporting and assurances to [REDACTED] regarding the AI’s compliance with contractual and regulatory requirements.

2.4 Where [REDACTED] does not approve:

(a) If [REDACTED] elects not to approve the new or changed use of AI proposed under clause 2.2, the Supplier must:

   (i) continue providing the Goods and Services in accordance with this Contract without incorporating or modifying AI beyond what was originally disclosed and approved by [REDACTED]; and

   (ii) not unilaterally introduce or amend the use of AI in any aspect of the Goods and Services without [REDACTED] express prior written consent.

(b) The Supplier shall not use [REDACTED] rejection of proposed AI as a basis for modifying, degrading, or limiting the quality or functionality of the Goods and Services.

SCHEDULE 7  
PRIVACY  

1. DEFINITIONS  

1.1 Definitions: In this Schedule, unless the context otherwise requires, the following definition applies:  

Privacy Breach has the meaning given to that term in the Privacy Act.  

2. PRIVACY  

2.1 Privacy: The Supplier shall comply with the Privacy Laws, and not do anything that would cause [redacted] to breach any Privacy Laws, including:  

(a) ensuring that the Goods and Services provided by the Supplier can be used by [redacted] in compliance with the Privacy Laws;  

(b) having a privacy policy in place that:  

   (i) includes appropriate measures in accordance with good industry practice to avoid the occurrence of, and minimise or mitigate, any Privacy Breach;  

   (ii) includes what to do if there is any Privacy Breach (including in the case of the Supplier, procedures to escalate to [redacted]); and  

   (iii) in the case of the Supplier, complies with all reasonable [redacted] policies and directions notified by [redacted] to the Supplier in relation to privacy; and  

(c) providing all information and assistance reasonably requested by [redacted] in order to facilitate compliance by [redacted] with its obligations under the Privacy Laws, including co-operating with any privacy assessment and completing privacy self-assessments at [redacted] request.  

2.2 Privacy Breach protocol: Without limiting the Supplier’s other obligations under this Contract, the Supplier will comply with the following privacy protocol:  

(a) The Supplier shall immediately:  

   (i) notify and escalate any Privacy Breach and/or near miss by the Supplier (or any of its Personnel or Subcontractors) relating to this Contract to [redacted] as soon as it becomes aware of such Privacy Breach or near miss; and  

   (ii) provide all information that [redacted] requests about any Privacy Breach and near miss(es) that could have led to a Privacy Breach that relate to this Contract.  

(b) The Supplier will work together with [redacted] to manage the consequences and implications of the Privacy Breach or near miss, including by:  
```

(i) making all such necessary Supplier Personnel available to [redacted] to assist [redacted] to manage the consequences and implications arising from the Privacy Breach or near miss;

(ii) complying with [redacted] issue management procedures;

(iii) following the process in section 113 of the Privacy Act to determine whether a Privacy Breach is a "notifiable privacy breach" (as defined in that Act) and, unless [redacted] notifies the Supplier that [redacted] will make the required notifications in respect of that Privacy Breach, make the required notifications as soon as reasonably practicable; and

(iv) implementing reasonable procedures to prevent such breaches or near misses in the future.

(c) The Supplier will not comment publicly (including to the media) about the Privacy Breach or near miss, without the prior written consent of [redacted] (at its sole discretion).

2.3 Declaration: The Supplier shall, at no less than 6 monthly intervals, provide [redacted] with a signed declaration confirming that the Supplier has complied with this clause 2 and has not exposed [redacted] to any undue risk around privacy requirements in the preceding 6 months.
